steps.title: Link steel type to use
steps.content: Storage containers use 300 series stainless steel, a subtype of austenitic stainless steel. The passage states that austenitic stainless steel has a face-centered cubic crystalline structure, unlike ferritic stainless steel used for induction cookers.

steps.title: Choose the supported contrast
steps.content: Choice A conflicts with ferritic steel's magnetic properties. Choice B invents a manganese threshold. Choice C matches the austenitic versus ferritic structural contrast. Choice D reverses the nickel relationship because nickel is added to make austenitic steel. The answer is C.
